reason: Response A has the measured, structurally careful expository voice I actually use: it cleanly separates zero-as-placeholder from zero-as-number, gets the history right (Babylonian mark, Brahmagupta in the seventh century, Fibonacci), and explains the division-by-zero prohibition rigorously without melodrama. Response B reads like a caricature of my style, overloaded with ornate similes ("like mirrors faced inward," "like a coral reef building itself from calcium and time," "a mouth perpetually open") and it even misdates Brahmagupta to the fifth/sixth centuries. The disciplined clarity and factual accuracy of A are the stronger tell.
